ddls:
CREATE TABLE "data_elements" (
  "data_element_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data element */,
  "path" TEXT /* Path of the data element */,
  "slice_name" TEXT /* Name of the slice */,
  "alias" TEXT /* Alias of the data element */,
  "label" TEXT /* Label of the data element */,
  "min" INTEGER /* Minimum value of the data element */,
  "max" INTEGER /* Maximum value of the data element */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the data element */,
  "short" TEXT /* Short description of the data element */,
  "definition" TEXT /* Definition of the data element */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments about the data element */,
  "data_standard_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data standard */,
  "data_format_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data format */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the data element (e.g., patient demographics, medical history) */,
  "is_required" INTEGER /* Indicate if the data element is required */,
  "is_deprecated" INTEGER /* Indicate if the data element is deprecated */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data element was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data element was last updated */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the data element */,
  "constraints" TEXT /* Additional constraints for the data element (e.g., regex patterns) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_element_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_elements_data_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_standard_id") REFERENCES data_standards ("data_standard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_elements_data_format_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_format_id") REFERENCES data_formats ("data_format_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_standards" (
  "data_standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data standard */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data standard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data standard */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the data standard */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data standard was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data standard was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicate if the data standard is active */,
  "documentation_url" TEXT /* URL to the official documentation for the data standard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_formats" (
  "data_format_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data format */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data format */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data format */,
  "data_standard_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data standard */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data format was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the data format was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicate if the data format is active */,
  "sample_data" TEXT /* Sample data in the format to help users understand the structure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_format_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_formats_data_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_standard_id") REFERENCES data_standards ("data_standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_element_relationships" (
  "relationship_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each relationship */,
  "data_element_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data element */,
  "related_data_element_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related data element */,
  "relationship_type" TEXT /* Type of relationship (e.g., parent-child, sibling) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the relationship was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the relationship was last updated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the relationship */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("relationship_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_element_relationships_related_data_element_id FOREIGN KEY ("related_data_element_id") REFERENCES data_elements ("data_element_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_element_relationships_data_element_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_element_id") REFERENCES data_elements ("data_element_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., healthcare professional, data analyst, administrator) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last updated */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicate if the user is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object containing user permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
